title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple discusses a revived murder case with Dr. Hedlock. Dr. Hedlock warns her against getting involved, advising her to "let the sleeping murder sleep" because killers who have successfully covered their tracks may react dangerously if an investigation resumes. Miss Marple explains her motive is not personal curiosity but a desire to look after two young, inexperienced children involved in the matter. Dr. Hedlock expresses concern that her involvement in Dillesham might be a "death sentence."

Upon arriving in Dillesham, Miss Marple visits her friends, Colonel and Mrs. Bantry. She finds the Colonel with a gun and a Spanish hound, while Mrs. Bantry is gardening. During their conversation, they discuss the Sanders, a former cook and her husband who used an inheritance to open an inn at Dillmouth Seafront, number 14. Mrs. Bantry encourages Miss Marple to stay there for the sea air and good food.

In a separate scene, Giles and Gwendolyn discuss a potential crime scene at a mountain villa. Gwendolyn recalls seeing a figure with non-human gray claws, but Giles remains skeptical, suggesting she is misremembering. Gwendolyn considers that the event might have been a childhood nightmare, noting that no one in Dillmouth has any record of a murder or disappearance occurring in that house. Giles reluctantly agrees it might be a dream, but he maintains his disbelief, arguing that one cannot dream specific lines from "The Duchess of Malfi."
